DESCRIPTION: Receive the incoming wafer lot, verify lot ID, product code, and route version.
Check wafer count, carrier ID, and traveler against the manufacturing order.
Inspect wafers for visible defects, edge chips, handling damage, and contamination.
Measure starting wafer thickness at center and edge locations.
Perform particle scan to quantify surface contamination before processing.
Remove loose particles and organic residues prior to wet processing.
Apply alkaline clean to remove organic contamination; monitor temperature and timing.
Apply acidic clean to remove metallic residues and remaining contaminants.
Remove native oxide with dilute HF and prepare a hydrogen-terminated surface.
Dry wafers using spin-rinse-dry or nitrogen drying to avoid water marks.
Verify substrate orientation, resistivity, and incoming material specification.
Grow a thin silicon oxide layer; control temperature, time, and target thickness.
Verify oxide thickness and uniformity with metrology.
Apply photoresist; track spin speed, resist thickness, and coating quality.
Remove solvent from the resist film and stabilize it for lithography.
Align the first lithography mask to the wafer using alignment marks.
Expose the resist with the first pattern; monitor dose and focus.
Develop the exposed resist to reveal the intended feature pattern.
Inspect pattern fidelity, CD variation, and defect density.
Etch oxide in the open regions with selectivity and endpoint control.
Remove remaining photoresist after etch.
Clean post-etch residues and particles from the surface.
Measure critical dimensions of etched openings and compare to target.
Implant well dopants with defined dose, energy, and tilt.
Check wafer condition and pattern integrity before anneal.
Drive dopants deeper into the substrate using a thermal diffusion step.
Activate implanted dopants and repair crystal damage.
Measure junction depth and profile across wafer locations.
Prepare the gate area with a final clean and surface conditioning.
Grow a thin, high-quality gate oxide with tight thickness control.
Measure gate oxide thickness and interface quality.
Deposit polysilicon gate material; monitor film thickness and uniformity.
Anneal polysilicon to improve film properties and reduce stress.
Verify polysilicon thickness and across-wafer uniformity.
Coat photoresist for gate patterning.
Soft bake resist before exposure.
Align the gate mask to the wafer and underlying structures.
Expose the gate pattern using the second mask.
Develop resist to define the gate geometry.
Inspect gate pattern for shorts, opens, and line-edge roughness.
Etch the polysilicon gate using a directional dry etch.
Remove resist after poly etch.
Clean etch residues and prepare for source/drain formation.
Measure gate critical dimension and overlay accuracy.
Implant source/drain dopants with controlled dose and energy.
Verify feature state before activation anneal.
Activate dopants and repair implant-induced damage.
Measure sheet resistance of source/drain regions to confirm activation.
Deposit dielectric spacer material with controlled conformality.
Etch spacer dielectric directionally to form sidewall spacers.
Measure spacer width and profile for process control.
Perform lightly doped drain implant to reduce electric field concentration.
Confirm wafer readiness before the second thermal step.
Activate LDD dopants with a short thermal cycle.
Verify dopant distribution and junction profile.
Deposit interlayer dielectric to isolate device layers and routing.
Densify dielectric by thermal treatment to improve film quality.
Measure dielectric thickness across wafer center and edge.
Polish dielectric to achieve surface planarization.
Measure surface planarity and residual topography after CMP.
Coat resist for contact/via patterning.
Soft bake resist before exposure.
Align contact/via mask to underlying device features.
Expose contact openings in the resist.
Develop resist to open contact locations.
Inspect via openings for CD, alignment, and completeness.
Etch dielectric to open vias to the device layer.
Remove photoresist after via etch.
Clean residues and prepare the surface for metallization.
Measure via critical dimension and verify target size.
Deposit barrier layer to improve adhesion and prevent diffusion.
Deposit seed layer for subsequent metal fill.
Fill vias with metal, controlling voids and coverage.
Polish excess metal and leave filled contacts flush with the surface.
Measure contact resistance to assess metallization quality.
Deposit first interconnect metal layer.
Anneal metal layer to improve stability and reduce stress.
Coat resist for metal routing patterning.
Soft bake resist before exposure.
Align metal routing mask.
Expose the first metal routing pattern.
Develop resist to define metal lines.
Inspect metal lines for opens, shorts, and CD variation.
Etch the metal according to the routing pattern.
Remove resist after metal etch.
Clean residue and particles after etching.
Measure line width and spacing.
Deposit passivation layer to protect the device from moisture and contamination.
Cure the passivation film to stabilize its properties.
Measure passivation thickness and coverage quality.
Open bond pad windows for probing and packaging.
Pattern resist for pad opening.
Develop resist to define pad openings.
Etch the passivation layer at pad sites.
Strip resist after pad opening.
Clean pad area to ensure low-resistance electrical access.
Verify pad opening dimensions and completeness.
Perform final clean before inspection and test.
Measure final wafer thickness and mechanical state.
Check bow, warp, and edge exclusion.
Perform final particle scan before electrical testing.
Run electrical parametric tests on the completed wafer.
Measure threshold voltage distribution across devices.
Check leakage current and isolate failing devices.
Evaluate switching behavior and key device dynamics.
Sort wafers into bins according to electrical results.
Analyze yield, defects, and any systematic process drift.
Release the lot after passing inspection and test criteria.
Prepare and ship the lot to downstream packaging or customer.
